signals.reasoning: BTC is liquid and stable, but the board remains no-trade and the active veto argues against opening fresh exposure. Neutral is still the right stance.
ETH has mildly constructive price action, but the board is still low-edge and not strong enough to justify a new position under veto conditions.
SOL has slightly better funding support than ETH and decent price action, but the board still does not give a strong enough edge to override the active veto.
TAO remains one of the cleaner bearish leans in the set with a negative 24h profile and a bearish mechanical board read. It is not a panic short, but short still looks better than long here.
CHILLGUY is moving hard, but the market is too thin, too wide, and too funding-rich on the long side to trust as a fresh continuation trade in a veto regime.
PYTH has acceptable liquidity and some constructive price action, but the board remains neutral and funding is positive. Interesting, not actionable.
KAITO still leans bearish with a large negative 24h move and a bearish board profile. The funding tailwind is weaker than before, so conviction is moderate rather than high.

MINA still has one of the strongest squeeze-continuation profiles on the board with deeply negative funding and bullish mechanics, but the spread is ugly and the active veto is the wrong environment to chase it.
GOAT remains a reasonable bearish lean because it is thin, unstable, and still not supported by the kind of funding structure that would make it a clean long.
TNSR still screens as a valid squeeze-continuation long mechanically, but the live veto is a direct reason not to open fresh high-beta exposure. Strong watchlist long, not an active trade.
DASH has strong price momentum and decent volume, but the board remains neutral and funding is positive. Watchlist only while veto is active.
